introSection: Mine Blower is a wooden hybrid roller coaster located at Fun Spot America in Kissimmee, Florida, USA. It was manufactured by the Gravity Group and is the first wooden roller coaster in the Southeast of America to feature an inversion.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The name was revealed to be Mine Blower over the radio on February 5, 2017. Due to the name, the ride was presumed to have a mining theme. On March 17, 2017, Fun Spot announced some new information on Mine Blower. The drop was announced as being at a 65° angle, the ride was heightened from to  and lengthened from  to. The ride was later opened on June 23.

The ride was temporarily closed spring of 2023 to be refurbished by Rocky Mountain Construction. The company's 208 ReTraK product was added to three sections of the ride. It reopened on June 12, 2023.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Mine Blower features a zero-g roll over the station and it also features a 115° over-banked curve.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: A piece of concept art that Fun Spot released on February 6, 2017 revealed that the ride's trains would have a TNT theming feature on the front.

fullText:
Mine Blower is a wooden hybrid roller coaster located at Fun Spot America in Kissimmee, Florida, USA. It was manufactured by the Gravity Group and is the first wooden roller coaster in the Southeast of America to feature an inversion.

The name was revealed to be Mine Blower over the radio on February 5, 2017. Due to the name, the ride was presumed to have a mining theme. On March 17, 2017, Fun Spot announced some new information on Mine Blower. The drop was announced as being at a 65° angle, the ride was heightened from to  and lengthened from  to. The ride was later opened on June 23.

The ride was temporarily closed spring of 2023 to be refurbished by Rocky Mountain Construction. The company's 208 ReTraK product was added to three sections of the ride. It reopened on June 12, 2023.

Mine Blower features a zero-g roll over the station and it also features a 115° over-banked curve.

A piece of concept art that Fun Spot released on February 6, 2017 revealed that the ride's trains would have a TNT theming feature on the front.
